// JV008 negative (origin gating): a secret-named argument is clean when the
// call it reaches is not a recognized capture sink. A same-named `error` on a
// non-slf4j type, a plain helper call, and a printing terminal are all outside
// the tier-1/tier-2 sink set, so JV008 does not broaden to them.
class Jv008NonSinkOrigin {
    void fakeLoggerIsNotASink(FakeLog log, String secret) {
        log.error("boom", secret);
    }

    void helperCallIsNotASink(String token) {
        process("boom", token);
    }

    void stderrPrintIsNotASink(String password) {
        System.err.println("boom " + password);
    }

    void process(String msg, String token) {}

    interface FakeLog {
        void error(String msg, String s);
    }
}
